nodes.label: AI as predictive model for extreme conditions
nodes.grounding_span: attempting to predict how these metals will react/perform in extreme situations that are difficult to recreate inhouse

nodes.label: AI as test case reviewer and regression testing aid
nodes.label_negative: Direct full-scale external testing without screening
nodes.grounding_span: use the AI to see if they are satisfactory and then send them off to facilities that can do the full testing as this is more efficient

nodes.label: AI optimization of physical process parameters
nodes.grounding_span: if an algorithm could optimize the temperature/pressure that the atomizer works at to produce more powder

nodes.label: AI usefulness for professional composition
nodes.grounding_span: AI giving us suggestions on what compositions to use

nodes.label: Workflow efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: more efficient than just sending a bunch of untested particles to be fully tested

nodes.label: Prediction accuracy for real-world performance
nodes.grounding_span: predict their effectiveness in realistic use cases

nodes.label: Reduction of human error and wasted effort
nodes.grounding_span: we get none or barely any powder from the run as some parameters were a little off

nodes.label: Positive toward AI for initial screening
Frustration with manual parameter setting and desire for AI optimization
nodes.grounding_span: Many times we will go through the long and tedious process to set up the machine and then we get none or barely any powder

nodes.label: Pragmatic cost-benefit analysis of AI use
nodes.grounding_span: it is not efficient to use AI in mechanical sieving or atomization as these both require physical labor and supervision

nodes.label: Sequential screening mindset — use AI to filter before committing resources
nodes.grounding_span: use the AI to see if they are satisfactory and then send them off to facilities that can do the full testing
